payload.description: A comprehensive record of genetic variations identified in tumor and normal samples, including genomic coordinates, gene annotations, and clinical significance.
Gene symbol as defined by the HUGO Gene Nomenclature Committee
Entrez gene numeric identifier
One or more genome sequencing centers where the variant was identified
The reference genome assembly version
The chromosome containing the variant
The lowest numeric position of the variant on the chromosome
The highest numeric position of the variant on the chromosome
The DNA strand the variant was found on
The functional effect of the variant on the protein coding sequence
Translational effect of the variant on the protein
Genetic category of the mutation
The nucleotide sequence found in the reference genome
Primary allele observed in the tumor sample
Secondary allele observed in the tumor sample
Reference SNP cluster ID from dbSNP
Validation status of the SNP as reported by dbSNP
Unique identifier for the tumor specimen barcode
Unique identifier for the normal control sample
Primary allele observed in the matched normal sample
Secondary allele observed in the matched normal sample
Validation allele observed in tumor samples via alternative methods
Validation allele observed in tumor samples via alternative methods
Validation allele observed in normal samples via alternative methods
Validation allele observed in normal samples via alternative methods
The status of the variant verification process
Indicates whether the mutation has been experimentally validated (e.g., Validated, Unknown, Untested).
The status of the mutation such as Somatic, Germline, or LOH.
The specific phase of the project or sequencing batch (e.g., Phase I, Phase II).
The type of material used for sequencing (e.g., PCR, Capture, WGS).
The platform or assay used to validate the identified mutation.
Internal scoring value assigned to the mutation call quality or impact.
The identifier or path of the sequence alignment file (BAM) used.
The model of the machine used to generate the sequences (e.g., Illumina HiSeq).
Number of reads supporting the reference allele in the tumor sample.
Number of reads supporting the variant (alternate) allele in the tumor sample.
Number of reads supporting the reference allele in the normal control sample.
Number of reads supporting the variant allele in the normal control sample.
DNA-level variant description following HGVS nomenclature.
Protein-level variant description following HGVS nomenclature.
Shortened format of the protein change description (e.g., p.V600E).
Ensembl or RefSeq transcript identifier.
Reference Sequence identifier for cDNA or protein.
Position of the mutation within the amino acid sequence.
The specific codon change associated with the mutation.
Binary flag (0 or 1) indicating if the mutation occurs in a known cancer hotspot.
Allele frequency in African American populations (often from ESP6500).
Allele frequency in African populations from 1000 Genomes project.
Numbering of alleles if multi-allelic site.
Allele frequency in Admixed American populations from 1000 Genomes project.
Allele frequency in Asian populations (East/South Asian) from 1000 Genomes project.
The alternative allele (variant nucleotide sequence) present at the specific genomic position.
The specific amino acid change resulting from the mutation in the protein sequence.
The biological classification of the transcript, such as protein coding or non-coding RNA.
Indicates if the transcript used for annotation is the representative (canonical) transcript.
Consensus Coding Sequence identifier for the mutation locus.
The position of the mutation within the coding sequence.
The list of sequencing centers or variant callers that identified this mutation.
The clinical significance of the variant based on ClinVar or similar databases.
The flanking sequence context surrounding the mutation site.
Catalogue Of Somatic Mutations In Cancer identification number.
Evidence supporting the variant from various databases such as 1000G or HapMap.
Distance from the variant to a genomic feature, such as a transcription start site.
Protein domains or functional motifs affected by the mutation.
The minor allele frequency of the variant in the East Asian population.
The minor allele frequency in European American populations (ESP).
The Ensembl stable ID for the protein product.
The minor allele frequency of the variant in the European population (1000 Genomes).
The specific exon number where the mutation occurs.
Global allele frequency from Exome Aggregation Consortium dataset.
Allele frequency in the African/African American population in ExAC.
Allele frequency in the Latino/Admixed American population in ExAC.
Allele frequency in the East Asian population in ExAC.
Allele frequency in the Finnish population in ExAC.
Allele frequency in the Non-Finnish European population in ExAC.
Allele frequency in other/unclassified populations in ExAC.
Allele frequency in the South Asian population from the Exome Aggregation Consortium dataset.
Identifier for previously documented variants such as dbSNP, COSMIC, or ClinVar IDs.
The quality control filter status of the variant call.
The stable identifier of the genomic feature (e.g., transcript ID).
The type of genomic feature annotated (e.g., Transcript, RegulatoryFeature).
Indicator of whether the gene is associated with a known phenotype/disease.
Global minor allele frequency in 1000 Genomes project.
The symbol or stable identifier for the mutated gene.
The unique ID provided by the HGNC (HUGO Gene Nomenclature Committee).
Shift measurement for HGVS nomenclature mapping.
Indicates if the mutation occurs in a high-information/conserved position.
Subjective classification of the severity of the variant's effect on the protein.

payload.label: High: Likely to cause protein truncation or loss-of-function
Low: Synonymous change or non-coding variant
Modifier: Usually non-coding variants with difficult to predict effects

payload.description: The intron number where the mutation is located.
The origin/database source of the merged variant record.
The name of the transcription factor binding motif overlapping the variant.
The position within the motif where the variant is located.
The change in position-weight matrix score for the motif caused by the variant.
The number of different variant calling algorithms that identified this mutation.
The phenotype status associated with this specific mutation variant.
Indicator of which transcript was picked as the most representative for summary.
Polymorphism Phenotyping score predicting effect of an amino acid substitution.
Minor allele frequency in the South Asian population.
Sorting Intolerant From Tolerant prediction score and label.
Indicates if the mutation is a somatic mutation (present only in tumor tissue).
The UniProtKB/Swiss-Prot accession number for the protein product.
The HGNC or official symbol for the gene associated with the mutation.
The database or resource providing the gene symbol.
The identifier for the translated protein product in the TrEMBL database.
The level of evidence supporting the existence of the transcript.
UniProt Archive identifier providing a non-redundant protein sequence database link.
The type of sequence variation observed.
Comprehensive list of functional consequences on affected transcripts.
The location of the mutation within the cDNA sequence.
The number of reads covering the variant site in the normal control sample.
The number of reads covering the variant site in the tumor sample.
Indicates if the variant annotation process completed successfully or failed.
